message:
feat(fcp-core,fwc): structured fork-resolution diagnostics, truthful preflight denial envelopes

fcp-core/connector_state:
- Add decision_detail field to ForkResolutionOutcome so audits and operator
  flows can read why a particular fork resolution was reached (lease-based,
  CRDT merge, manual, or failure) without source-code archaeology
- Add MergeDiagnostic struct capturing strategy, branch sizes, merged size,
  and conflict count for CRDT merge explanations
- Adjust success()/failure() constructors to populate decision_detail
  automatically from the resolution strategy

fwc/main — CommandEnvelope truthfulness fix:
- Always inject CommandEnvelope into the dispatch payload, even when preflight
  denies execution. Previously denied dispatches omitted the envelope entirely,
  leaving the structured output without provenance metadata. Now denied
  dispatches carry CommandAvailability::Denied so consumers always see the
  decision and can distinguish "denied before execution" from "missing data"
- Fix invoke_dispatch_host to use the actual command name instead of
  hardcoded "invoke" string for the live envelope

fwc/intent:
- Add #[allow(dead_code)] attributes on IntentProvenance, IntentRefusalReason,
  LogTailContract, and LifecycleIntentContract to suppress warnings during
  incremental compilation while these types are being wired into the CLI
- Minor formatting cleanup (rustfmt line-length adjustments)

Operator truth contract:
- Expand fixture_matrix.json with sixth answer class "refusal" covering
  truthful preflight denial, plus replay contract fields (rerun command,
  required artifact bundle members, required structured log fields)
- Extend cual_integration.rs with refusal flow coverage (+204 lines)
- Update FWC_Host_First_Truthfulness_Playbook.md to document the refusal
  class and per-row replay contract requirements

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

+      "error_type": "policy-denied",
+      "preflight_allowed": false,
+      "minimum_next_actions": 2,
+      "notes": "Truthful refusal scenarios must freeze the denied preflight envelope without fabricating runtime success, while preserving the concrete remediation commands that operators can actually run next."
+    },

@@ -283,15 +283,22 @@ This bead is the documentation layer of a wider evidence stack. The sibling bead
 
 The canonical fixture set for stable operator-truth answers now lives in `crates/fwc/testdata/operator_truth/fixture_matrix.json`.
 
-It freezes five representative answer classes that downstream CLI work can depend on without re-reading implementation details:
+It freezes six representative answer classes spanning the five required operator-truth modes, so downstream CLI work can depend on one shared acceptance matrix without re-reading implementation details:
 
 - `offline`: manifest-backed `show --offline` answers
 - `node_local`: host-admin `status <connector> --host ...` answers
 - `mesh_backed`: live `mesh explain-availability` answers with placement-backed readiness
 - `degraded`: live `health <connector> --host ...` answers where degradation is explicit
-- `fallback_derived`: live `install` answers where host mutation succeeded but post-install truth is still degraded
+- `fallback_derived`: the degraded post-mutation install case, where host mutation succeeded but post-install truth is still degraded
+- `refusal`: truthful preflight denial for live `invoke` when policy rejects execution before the connector runs
 
-`crates/fwc/tests/cual_integration.rs` executes those representative flows against the fixture matrix, so changes to operator-facing truth fields must update both the implementation and the frozen contract fixture set.
+Each matrix row also freezes the minimum replay contract for later proof-bundle work:
+
+- the representative rerun command
+- the required artifact bundle members
+- the required structured log fields
+
+`crates/fwc/tests/cual_integration.rs` executes the representative flows against the fixture matrix, so changes to operator-facing truth fields or evidence requirements must update both the implementation and the frozen contract fixture set.
 
 ### Fast semantic checks
